behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert very specific patterns like 'head or self.head' as literal strings in source code (test_publish_format_uses_self_head, test_push_to_hub_uses_self_head). The instruction.md says 'fall back to the instance attribute value' but never specifies the exact pattern 'head or self.head' that tests grep for. Also, tests check specific file paths like '.agents/skills/gradio/SKILL.md' and 'guides/03_building-with-blocks/06_custom-HTML-components.md' and specific content patterns (e.g., 'Chart.j

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states implementation details: 'Add head: str | None = None to the HTML.__init__ signature, positioned before server_functions', 'Store the head value on the instance', 'these methods should fall back to the instance attribute value set during construction'. It also specifies exact parameter positioning and the fallback pattern. This goes beyond describing what's broken — it prescribes the exact fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: test_publish_format_uses_self_head checks for the literal string 'head or self.head' in source code, test_push_to_hub_uses_self_head similarly checks for 'head or self.head'. An alternative correct implementation using e.g. 'self.head if not head else head' or a helper method would fail these tests despite being functionally equivalent.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_head_param_in_init, test_head_stored_as_attribute, test_publish_format_uses_self_head, test_push_to_hub_uses_self_head, test_skill_md_head_param, test_guide_head_section) are ALL source-text/AST inspection tests that never actually import or execute the HTML component. They parse source code and grep for string patterns like 'head or self.head', 'Chart.js', etc. No test actually instantiates gr.HTML with head= and verifies runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: While the tests are text-based, they do check for specific non-default content that wouldn't exist in a stub. A stub implementation wouldn't have 'self.head = head' in __init__ or 'head or self.head' in _to_publish_format, so these tests would fail on unmodified code.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is available at /workspace/gradio after applying solve.sh, and the tests only check source text patterns. An agent could read /tmp/patch1.diff or the solution directory to extract exact strings. Also, since tests grep for literal patterns like 'head or self.head', an agent could simply insert those exact strings without understanding the feature.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires that 'When that argument is falsy or not provided, these methods should fall back to the instance attribute value' — but no test actually calls _to_publish_format or push_to_hub to verify the fallback behavior at runtime. Only source text inspection is performed.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear sections (Gates, Pass-to-pass gates, Fail-to-pass), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB outputs.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names the exact file (gradio/components/html.py), the exact parameter position ('positioned before server_functions'), and the exact fallback mechanism ('fall back to the instance attribute value'). It also names the specific methods (_to_publish_format and push_to_hub implicitly via 'publishing and Hub-upload methods'). This is excessive localization for what should be a design task.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is not an agentmd-edits task; it involves code and documentation changes.
